title: Cargo Book: Package Layout and Workspaces
snippet: Cargo workspaces let multiple packages share a lockfile, output directory, and dependency configuration.
body: Cargo workspaces let multiple packages share a lockfile, output directory, and dependency configuration. They are a good fit for multi-service Rust platforms where API, crawler, and indexing components evolve together.

title: Next.js Route Handlers
snippet: Route Handlers allow you to create custom request handlers using the Web Request and Response APIs.
body: Next.js Route Handlers allow you to create custom request handlers using the Web Request and Response APIs. They fit well when a frontend needs lightweight proxy endpoints for developer portal actions or auth-aware requests.

title: MDN: HTTP Caching
snippet: HTTP caching stores a response associated with a request and reuses the stored response for subsequent requests.
body: HTTP caching stores a response associated with a request and reuses the stored response for subsequent requests. Search engines rely on freshness strategies, conditional requests, and cache validators to reduce crawl cost while keeping results current.

title: OpenSearch Overview
snippet: OpenSearch provides distributed search and analytics with indexing, ranking, filtering, and aggregation primitives.
body: OpenSearch provides distributed search and analytics with indexing, ranking, filtering, and aggregation primitives. It is a common choice for early search platforms that need lexical relevance, filtering, and scalable index writes before bespoke ranking systems arrive.

title: Redpanda for Event Streaming Pipelines
snippet: Redpanda is a streaming platform compatible with Kafka APIs and often used to decouple ingestion pipelines.
body: Redpanda is a streaming platform compatible with Kafka APIs and often used to decouple ingestion pipelines. In a search stack it can buffer discovery events, fetch jobs, parser outputs, and indexing updates with clearer failure isolation.

title: Robots Exclusion Protocol
snippet: robots.txt tells crawlers which paths are allowed, disallowed, or governed by crawl-delay and related directives.
body: robots.txt tells crawlers which paths are allowed, disallowed, or governed by crawler directives. A compliant search engine must check robots before fetching, respect noindex controls, and keep override tooling for takedown requests.

title: ClickHouse for Search Analytics
snippet: ClickHouse is optimized for analytical workloads and is well suited to query logs, click logs, and ranking diagnostics.
body: ClickHouse is optimized for analytical workloads and is well suited to query logs, click logs, and ranking diagnostics. Search teams often use it to evaluate relevance shifts and hotspot traffic after ranking changes.

title: MinIO Object Storage for Crawl Artifacts
snippet: Object storage is a natural fit for raw HTML captures, parser output blobs, and archival snapshots.
body: Object storage is a natural fit for raw HTML captures, parser output blobs, and archival snapshots. A search crawler can persist fetch payloads and metadata before parse and dedup stages consume them.

title: Designing Ranking Systems for Search
snippet: Practical ranking starts with lexical retrieval, field boosts, domain quality, and freshness decay before learned ranking layers.
body: Practical ranking starts with lexical retrieval, field boosts, domain quality, and freshness decay before learned ranking layers. For an initial beta search engine, deterministic scoring keeps debugging and quality review tractable.

title: Operating a Developer API Key Platform
snippet: API key systems need creation, revocation, usage accounting, rate limits, and clear developer documentation.
body: API key systems need creation, revocation, usage accounting, rate limits, and clear developer documentation. For a public beta, transparent limits and fast revocation matter more than sophisticated billing.

title: Managing Crawl Frontier Queues
snippet: A frontier decides which URLs to fetch next based on freshness, host politeness, priority, and failure backoff.
body: A frontier decides which URLs to fetch next based on freshness, host politeness, priority, and failure backoff. Seed files, sitemaps, and page link extraction all contribute candidates into the same scheduling system.

title: Multilingual Search Basics
snippet: Language detection and per-language analyzers matter when a search product targets a global web corpus.
body: Language detection and per-language analyzers matter when a search product targets a global web corpus. Even before advanced NLP, tagging content language enables cleaner filtering and result diversification.
